summary: Regression check after design system update (Sora font + crimson/steel-grey palette + new black-sidebar Admin Shell). Backend: 26/26 pytest pass (20 existing + 6 new for /api/admin/dashboard, /api/admin/attendance/trend, /api/admin/attendance/by-department, /api/attendance/admin/today, /api/attendance/today, /api/attendance/history). Frontend: Login (staff role) loads with new crimson/white palette, demo card → faculty login works, mobile Home renders cleanly, Check-in Now navigates to /modules/attendance, multi-punch single-toggle button flips state correctly (action-out → action-in after a successful punch, hero badge updated to 5 PUNCHES). Desktop Admin Portal /admin (1600×950) verified: black sidebar #0F0F12 confirmed via computed bg rgb(15,15,18); all 5 nav items present (data-testid='nav-dashboard|attendance|employees|geofences|reports'); active nav crimson left-bar rendered (rgb(220,20,60), w3×h28); 'Front Desk · Gate 1' footer card; top breadcrumb 'HR > Overview' + big 'Dashboard' title; search bar with ⌘K, bell with red dot, user pill with PS initials avatar; all 6 KPI cards present with testIDs (kpi-total/present/in/inside/out/late), CURRENTLY INSIDE shows 'LIVE' tag, LATE ARRIVALS has pink tint; Recent Staff Activity table with date + filter pills; Late Arrivals side panel shows green check 'All staff arrived on time today'; Attendance Trend + By Department chart cards render. All 4 admin sub-pages (/admin/attendance, /admin/employees, /admin/geofences, /admin/reports) load without errors. CRITICAL DESIGN REGRESSION: Sora font is NOT being applied to rendered DOM — @font-face for Sora-Regular/Medium/SemiBold/Bold/ExtraBold is registered (7 rules), but every rendered Text element has computed fontFamily '-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI, Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif' (RN-Web default) and ZERO elements carry a Sora inline style. The global Text.render patch in src/font.ts is not taking effect on web. Deprecated packages detected: none new. RN-Web deprecation warnings still present (shadow*, props.pointerEvents) — same as iteration_1.

ui_bugs.issue: Sora @font-face is loaded but NOT applied to any Text element. All rendered text falls back to RN-Web's system font stack. Verified via getComputedStyle on multiple elements (Hello Dr., Mark Your Attendance, Dashboard, Total Staff, Recent Staff Activity) — fontFamily is '-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI...' and document has 0 elements with inline 'Sora' style. ROOT CAUSE: src/font.ts patches `Text.render` on the imported React Native Text component, but on react-native-web Text is a memoized forwardRef whose render fn doesn't propagate the prepended style through RN-Web's StyleSheet flattening (fontFamily applied via `style` prop is overridden by the css class `css-text-146c3p1`'s cascade because RN-Web generates a class per-style key and the patched fontFamily is added at the OUTER end of the style array AFTER user styles, so RN-Web's StyleSheet.flatten picks it up but the final emitted CSS class for fontFamily is missing — the patched object is added but the existing className already wins for fontFamily resolution because RN-Web atomic CSS uses class precedence, not inline style). Also `Text.defaultProps` is deprecated in React 18 functional components.

issues: Sidebar brand subtitle reads 'VMS · RECEPTION' and the user-pill subtitle reads 'Receptionist · Gate 1' / 'Front Desk · Gate 1' — these are leftover from a Visitor Management Shell. For an HR Admin Portal they should likely be 'HR PORTAL' and 'Administrator' (the user's role).

Top breadcrumb on sub-pages shows 'VMS > Overview' (default fallback) instead of an 'HR > <SectionName>' breadcrumb. Only /admin Dashboard passes breadcrumb='HR'. Sub-pages should pass appropriate breadcrumb values to AdminShell.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warnings persist: 'shadow*' style props are deprecated, use 'boxShadow'. Triggered by theme tokens that still use shadowColor/shadowOffset/shadowOpacity/shadowRadius (clay.surface/surfaceSoft/crimson/hero in /app/frontend/src/theme.ts L134-208). Already noted in iteration_1.
Console warnings persist: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Same as iteration_1 — unresolved.

applyGlobalFontPatch() in /app/frontend/src/font.ts:23-62 does not work on react-native-web — Text component is a memoized forwardRef and the render-patch + defaultProps approach is no longer effective in modern RN-Web. Recommended fix: inject a global CSS rule like `body, .css-text-146c3p1, [role="text"] { font-family: 'Sora-Regular', -apple-system, ...; }` from inside applyGlobalFontPatch via document.head.insertRule, OR set the platform-specific webpack/metro alias to provide a custom Text wrapper, OR set the RN-Web build setting `setTextStyle` baseline. Reference: /app/frontend/src/font.ts:23-62.

action_items: [HIGH] Sora font is loaded but not applied — global Text.render patch in src/font.ts has no effect on react-native-web. Suggested fix: inside applyGlobalFontPatch() inject a global CSS rule via document.head (web only) that sets font-family: 'Sora-Regular', system-ui, ... on `body, .css-text-146c3p1, input, textarea` so the @font-face rules already loaded actually take effect. Also use weight-mapped CSS (e.g., .css-text-fw-700 { font-family: 'Sora-Bold' }).

[MEDIUM] Update AdminShell sidebar brand sub-text from 'VMS · RECEPTION' to 'HR PORTAL' and user pill role from 'Receptionist · Gate 1' to the user's actual role/title to match the redesigned HR admin context. File: /app/frontend/src/components/AdminShell.tsx L90, L172.

[MEDIUM] Sub-pages (/admin/attendance, /admin/employees, /admin/geofences, /admin/reports) should pass `breadcrumb='HR'` (and appropriate subtitle) to AdminShell — currently they fall back to the default 'VMS' breadcrumb.

[LOW / carryover from iteration_1] Replace `shadow*` props with `boxShadow` in /app/frontend/src/theme.ts (clay.* recipes) and move `pointerEvents` into `style` to silence RN-Web deprecation warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: src/font.ts:23-62 — Patching React.forwardRef.render directly is fragile and known to not work reliably across React Native Web versions. The `defaultProps` mutation also triggers React 18 deprecation warnings for functional components. A better pattern is exporting a wrapper `<MUText>` from a single module and using that everywhere, OR injecting global CSS @ web boot.

/app/frontend/src/components/AdminShell.tsx L82-133 — Sidebar text still references the old VMS layout ('VMS · RECEPTION', 'Front Desk · Gate 1', 'Receptionist · Gate 1'). Either parameterize these via props or update for HR portal context.
/app/backend/server.py — unchanged this iteration; previous note still stands (file is 1388 lines, should be split).

All admin endpoints (/api/admin/dashboard, /api/admin/attendance/trend, /api/admin/attendance/by-department, /api/attendance/admin/today) return clean JSON with correct shapes and no MongoDB _id leakage — verified by test_admin_dashboard_endpoints.py.

success_rate.frontend: 85% (login + multi-punch + admin shell + all 6 KPIs + sub-pages OK; Sora font failure prevents 100%)

seed_data_creation: No new seed users. Faculty added a couple of attendance punches during testing (5 total punches today by end of run — already 4 going in).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest files: test_attendance_e2e.py (20 tests, multi-punch + auth + admin) and test_admin_dashboard_endpoints.py (6 tests, new admin dashboard endpoints). Run with: EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=https://campus-hub-453.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Frontend: the Sora font regression is verifiable in browser console via `getComputedStyle(document.querySelector('.css-text-146c3p1')).fontFamily` — if it does NOT include 'Sora', the patch is still broken. To verify a fix worked, also confirm `Array.from(document.fonts).filter(f=>f.family.includes('Sora') && f.status==='loaded').length >= 1` (fonts need to actually load, not just be declared).
rca_of_issue: Sora font regression RCA: applyGlobalFontPatch() in /app/frontend/src/font.ts attempts to wrap `Text.render` to prepend fontFamily into the rendered React element's style. On react-native-web (recent versions), `Text` is a memoized forwardRef whose internal render returns a host component (`div`/`span`) wrapped by RN-Web's `createElement` — which routes the style through StyleSheet.flatten and converts it into atomic CSS classes. Prepending `{fontFamily:'Sora-Regular'}` at the START of the style array means user styles (including the absent fontFamily) are flattened AFTER, but because there is NO user-level fontFamily anywhere, the result SHOULD have fontFamily Sora-Regular… in theory. In practice we observe computed fontFamily is the system fallback and 0 elements carry an inline 'Sora' style — meaning either (a) the patch never executes because `Text.render` is undefined for the memo(forwardRef) export, or (b) RN-Web's StyleSheet.flatten doesn't pick up the prepended object because the patch runs AFTER the StyleSheet cache is populated. Fix: web-only, inject a global CSS rule via document.head.appendChild(styleTag) that targets the RN-Web text class (.css-text-146c3p1) and inputs with `font-family: 'Sora-Regular', system-ui, ...`. Apply weight variants via separate rules on .r-fontWeight-* class names.
